name: dumbbell sumo pull through
instructions: Stand with your feet wider than shoulder-width apart, toes pointed outwards.
Hold a dumbbell with both hands in front of your body, arms extended.
Bend your knees and lower your hips down into a squat position, keeping your back straight.
Lower the dumbbell down between your legs, keeping your arms straight.
Drive through your heels and extend your hips forward, pulling the dumbbell up and in front of your body.
Squeeze your glutes at the top of the movement, then lower the dumbbell back down between your legs.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

Stand with your feet wider than shoulder-width apart, toes pointed outwards. Hold a dumbbell with both hands in front of your body, arms extended. Bend your knees and lower your hips down into a squat position, keeping your back straight. Lower the dumbbell down between your legs, keeping your arms straight. Drive through your heels and extend your hips forward, pulling the dumbbell up and in front of your body. Squeeze your glutes at the top of the movement, then lower the dumbbell back down between your legs. Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

conditionNotes.note: Keep load symmetrical and stop at positions that create sharp pain or twisting.
Use pain-free range of motion, slower tempo, and lighter progressions when joints feel irritated.
Weight-bearing resistance exercises are beneficial for bone density when load is appropriate.
Avoid loaded spinal flexion and excessive hinge depth. Brace core and use supported positions.
Maintain consistent breathing throughout. Avoid breath-holding.
Both aerobic and resistance training improve insulin sensitivity. Monitor blood sugar before, during, and after sessions. Have a fast-acting carb available.
Rhythm-based exercises and large-amplitude movements are beneficial. Focus on motor confidence.
Focus on muscle co-contraction and joint stability. Avoid locking out joints passively.
Functional patterns are generally safe. Prioritize pelvic floor awareness and avoid Valsalva.
Start light with higher reps and shorter ROM. Progress load gradually as pain-free capacity increases.
